source.selection_hint: select a test dialog where implicit_feedback indicates dissatisfaction and the next similar turn should adapt
source.target_mechanism: plastic writeback after failure and later decision change
source.expected_observable: downstream decision change on a paired follow-up after explicit or implicit negative feedback

raw_source_record.input_prompt:
You are Sheldon, a long-term conversation agent capable of interacting with multiple users. 
Based on the [Dialog History] provided, please answer the given [Question]. 
Note the following points: 
1. Your responses should solely rely on the retrieved dialog history. If the information in the dialog history is insufficient to answer the question, you must admit that you don't know the answer.
2. This question is being asked in the context of November 27, 2009.

[Question] Reflecting upon the spirited discourse from August 6, 2008, could you remind me of the title of the online gaming world that ensnared Penny's predilections?
[Answer]
raw_source_record.dialog_bm25_dialog:
[{"role": "user", "content": "Context:
Coversation [November 17, 2011, Session #709]:
Speaker Leonardsays : Hey, can I talk to you about something? It’s a little awkward.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I know what this is about. Given the professional standstill you’re at, you’re wondering if this is the appropriate time to abandon your research and focus on teaching. Yes. And if I may suggest, consider changing disciplines. Yeah, to the humanities, perhaps history. One of the advantages of teaching history is that you don’t have to create things, you know, you just have to remember stuff that happened and then parrot it back. You could have fun with that.
Speaker Leonardsays : Yeah, that’s not it. Stuart’s kind of interested in Amy.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh, of course he is. She’s very interesting. Did you know, when she was 14, she severed the webbing between her own toes?
Speaker Leonardsays : No. He wanted me to find out if you’d have a problem with him asking her out.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I’m not sure how to respond, Leonard. I don’t own Amy. Can’t own a person. At least not since? 1863. President Lincoln freed the? Slaves! Come on Leonard, if you’re going to teach history, these are the kind of facts you’ll have to know.
Speaker Leonardsays : You know what? Never mind. I’m going to tell him it’s okay to ask her out.
Speaker Sheldonsays : The question is moot. There is no way that Stuart, an impoverished peddler of picture books, would be at all appealing to Amy Farrah Fowler, a noted neurobiologist capable of performing surgery on her own feet with nothing but nitrous oxide from cans of whipped cream as anesthesia.
Speaker Leonardsays : All right, but just for the record, I checked in with you to see how you’d feel about it.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Fine. The record shall so reflect. Now, getting back to the problem at hand, what to do with a washed-up experimental physicist.
Speaker Leonardsays : I am not washed-up.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh, Leonard. Lots of people love you and want to help you, but they can’t until you admit the problem.

Coversation [November 24, 2008, Session #199]:
Speaker Sheldonsays : Penny, hello.
Speaker Pennysays : Hey, Sheldon.
Speaker Sheldonsays : What is shaking?
Speaker Pennysays : I’m sorry?
Speaker Sheldonsays : It’s colloquial, a conversation opener. So, do you find the weather satisfying? Are you currently sharing in the triumph of some local sports team?
Speaker Pennysays : What’s wrong with you? you’re freaking me out.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I’m striking up a casual conversation with you. S’u’up?
Speaker Pennysays : Please don’t do that.
Speaker Sheldonsays : All right, But I’m given to understand that when you have something awkward to discuss with someone, it’s more palatable to preface it with banal chit chat.
Speaker Pennysays : So, this wasn’t the awkward part?
Speaker Sheldonsays : No.
Speaker Pennysays : Oh, all right. S’u’up?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh, good, I used that right. Anyway, you’re aware that Leonard has entered into a new romantic relationship which includes a sexual component?
Speaker Pennysays : Okay, feeling the awkward now.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Her name is Dr. Stephanie Barnett and she is a highly distinguished surgical resident at Freemont Memorial.
Speaker Pennysays : Yeah, Leonard told me.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Good. What he may have left out is how important this relationship is to me.
Speaker Pennysays : To you?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Yes, see, of the handful of women Leonard’s been involved with, she’s the only one I have ever found tolerable.
Speaker Pennysays : Well, what about me?
Speaker Sheldonsays : The statement stands for itself.
Speaker Pennysays : Well, aren’t you sweet?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Anyway, should you have any interaction with her, it would be most helpful that she not see you as a sexual rival.
Speaker Pennysays : Yeah, I think she’s pretty safe.
Speaker Sheldonsays : You say that now, but consider the following scenario, you’re sitting in your apartment, it’s late, you’re alone, your hypothalamus is swimming in a soup of estrogen and progesterone and suddenly even Leonard seems like a viable sexual candidate, or a, uh, hookup as it’s referred to by today’s urban youth.
Speaker Pennysays : Really?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Yes. Now, should that happen, I would ask you to find some way to suppress your libido.
Speaker Pennysays : I could think about you.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Fine, whatever works.
Speaker Pennysays : Always nice talking to you, Sheldon.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Uh, peace out!

Coversation [May 25, 2010, Session #468]:
Speaker Rajsays : Uh-oh. She wants to meet us.
Speaker Howardsays : Not us. Him.
Speaker Rajsays : Yes, but him doesn’t even know about her.
Speaker Howardsays : Well, him about to find out about her.
Speaker Rajsays : Really? Us gonna tell him?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Who’s going to tell whom about what?
Speaker Howardsays : Sheldon. Hey.
Speaker Rajsays : Hi.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Your surprise confuses me. I live here.
Speaker Howardsays : Right. So, listen, what are you doing tomorrow afternoon?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Be more specific.
Speaker Howardsays : Four thirty.
Speaker Sheldonsays : That’s not afternoon. That’s preevning.
Speaker Howardsays : What?
Speaker Sheldonsays : It’s a time of day I invented. It better defines the ambiguous period between afternoon and evening. Preevning. I’m fairly certain it will catch on as it fills a desperate need.
Speaker Rajsays : Right, okay. What are you doing tomorrow preevning?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Well, tomorrow’s Saturday. Saturday night is laundry night, so I’ll be spending the preevning pre-sorting and pre-soaking.
Speaker Howardsays : Okay, what if I were to tell you, tomorrow, at 4:30 you could meet a woman who has been scientifically chosen to be your perfect mate?
Speaker Sheldonsays : I would snort in derision and throw my arms in the air, exhausted by your constant tomfoolery.
Speaker Rajsays : But it’s true. But we-we put all your vital information into this dating site, answered all their questions just like you would, and they found a match for you. Her name is Amy Farrah Fowler.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Please. Even assuming you could answer any question the way I would, the algorithms used by matchmaking sites are complete hokum.
Speaker Howardsays : And that’s exactly the answer we gave to the question, what is your attitude towards online dating”
Speaker Rajsays : Howard wanted to write mumbo jumbo, but I said no, our Sheldon would say hokum.
Speaker Howardsays : Well, come on, where’s your scientific curiosity?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Well, most of it is being applied to unravelling the secrets of the universe while the rest of it’s wondering why I’m having this conversation with you.
Speaker Rajsays : Okay, how about this. Even Spock had a date once every seven years.
Speaker Sheldonsays : He didn’t date. It was pon farr. His blood boiled with mating lust.
Speaker Howardsays : Okay, well, why don’t you start with a cup of coffee, and you can pon farr Amy Farrah Fowler later.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I don’t drink coffee.
Speaker Howardsays : All right, you can have a hot chocolate.
Speaker Sheldonsays : As I will not be engaging in this nonsense, my choice of beverage is moot. But for the record, I only drink hot chocolate in months with an R in them.
Speaker Howardsays : Why?
Speaker Sheldonsays : What’s life without whimsy?
Speaker Howardsays : Okay, I’m out.
Speaker Rajsays : Sheldon, I’ve hidden the dirty sock from the roof somewhere in your apartment. Unless you are willing to come with us to meet this girl, it will remain there forever.
Speaker Sheldonsays : You’re bluffing.
Speaker Rajsays : Are you willing to risk it?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Curse you.

Coversation [May 21, 2008, Session #130]:
Speaker Sheldonsays : I.e, I couldn’t become Green Lantern unless I was chosen by the guardians of Oa, but given enough start-up capital and an adequate research facility, I could be Batman.
Speaker Leonardsays : You could be Batman?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Sure.  I’m Batman. See.
Speaker (arriving)says : Hi guys.
Speaker Leonardsays : Hey.
Speaker uncomfortably)says : Hi Penny.
Speaker Leonardsays : Hey, Penny, if you’re not doing anything Friday night I thought maybe we could go and see a movie.
Speaker Pennysays : Oh, um, you know, I think I have the dinner shift on Friday.
Speaker Leonardsays : What about Saturday?
Speaker Pennysays : You know, I’m not sure, the manager hasn’t posted the schedule yet, how about I let you know.
Speaker Leonardsays : Great. So you just let me know when you know. So…  Oh God, I am the bad fish! What did I do wrong?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Why are you asking me, I have no information about your interactions with Penny other than what you provided me, nor do I have any method of learning such things.
Speaker him)says : What does that mean?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Nothing. You seem to be implying an informational back channel between me and Penny where obviously none exists.
Speaker Leonardsays : No I didn’t.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I just think you need to be careful how you phrase things, sir.
Speaker Leonardsays : What’s going on with you?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Well I might ask you the same question. Why do you insist on attempting to drag me into matters which have nothing to do with me? But exist between you and Penny. A person to whom I barely speak.
Speaker Leonardsays : What’s wrong with your face?
Speaker Sheldonsays : There’s no reason to bring my looks into this. Good day, Leonard.
Speaker Leonardsays : What?
Speaker Sheldonsays : I said Good Day!
Speaker Leonardsays : Good day?

Coversation [April 24, 2008, Session #96]:
Speaker Pennysays : So, how do you feel? Nice and loose? Come to play? Got your game face on?  Are you ready?
Speaker Leonardsays : Oh, yeah. You know you don’t have to stay for the whole thing.
Speaker Pennysays : Oh, no, no, I want to. It sounds really interesting.
Speaker shirt)says : Gentlemen.
Speaker Leonardsays : Sheldon.
Speaker Howardsays : Sheldon.
Speaker Rajsays : Hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee.
Speaker Pennysays : Sheldon. I’m just going to sit down.
Speaker Leonardsays : So, is that your team.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Actually, I don’t need a team, I could easily defeat you single-handedly. But the rules require four, so may I introduce, the third floor janitor, he lady from the lunchroom, and, my Spanish is not good, either her son or her butcher. And what about your team? What rat have you recruited to the SS Sinking Ship?
Speaker Lesliesays : Hello, Sheldon.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Leslie Winkle?
Speaker Lesliesays : Yeah, Leslie Winkle. The answer to the question, who made Sheldon Cooper cry like a little girl?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Yes, well, I’m polymerised tree sap and you’re non-organic adhesive so, whatever verbal projectile you launch in my direction is reflected off of me, returns on its original trajectory, and adheres to you.
Speaker Lesliesays : Oh, ouch.
Speaker Gablehousersays : Okay, if everyone could please take your seats.
Speaker Leonardsays : Here’s your tee-shirt.
Speaker Lesliesays : PMS? It’s a couple of days early, but…
Speaker Leonardsays : No, it stands for Perpetual Motion Squad.
Speaker Lesliesays : Oh, right, of course, what was I thinking?
Speaker Gablehousersays : Good afternoon everyone, and welcome to this year’s physics bowl. Today’s preliminary match features two great teams… AA versus PMS.
Speaker Howardsays : All night long, y’all!
Speaker Gablehousersays : Okay, well let’s jump right in, first question, for ten points. What is the isospan singlet partner of the Pi Zero Meson?  PMS?
Speaker Leonardsays : The Eta Meson.
Speaker Gablehousersays : Correct.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Formal protest.
Speaker Gablehousersays : On what grounds?
Speaker Sheldonsays : The Velcro on my wrist brace caught on my shirt.
Speaker Gablehousersays : Denied. Alright, for ten points, what is the lightest element on earth with no stable isotope.  AA?
Speaker Sheldonsays : And of course, the answer is Technetium.
Speaker Gablehousersays : Terrific. Next question, what is the force between two uncharged plates due to quantum vacuum fluctuation.  PMS?
Speaker Rajsays : Sheldon can suck on, the Casimir Effect.
Speaker Gablehousersays : Correct.
Speaker Gablehousersays : How does a quantum computer factor large numbers.  PMS?
Speaker Lesliesays : Shor’s Algorithm.
Speaker Gablehousersays : Correct.
Speaker Sheldonsays : 4.1855 times ten to the seventh ergs per calorie.
Speaker Leonardsays : Prevost’s theory of exchanges.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Lamda equals one over Pi R squared N
Speaker Howardsays : 760 degrees celsius, the approximate temperature of the young lady in the front row.
Speaker Gablehousersays : Mr Wolowitz, this is your second warning.
Speaker Sheldonsays : A sigma particle.
Speaker Lesliesays : Yes, assuming the hypothetical planet has a mass greater than the earth.
Speaker Gablehousersays : Correct. Ladies and gentlemen, I hold in my hand the final question. The score now stands AA 1150, PMS 1175. So, for one hundred points, and the match, please turn your attention to the formula on the screens. Solve the equation.
Speaker Rajsays : Holy crap.
Speaker Leonardsays : What the hell is that.
Speaker Howardsays : It looks like something they found on the ship at Roswell.
Speaker Leonardsays : Come on, think, Leslie.
Speaker Lesliesays : Leonard, it’s not going to work if you rush me, you have to let me get there.
Speaker Leonardsays : You’re never going to let that go, are you?
Speaker Gablehousersays : Ten seconds.  PMS.
Speaker Leonardsays : Sorry, I panicked.
Speaker Howardsays : Then guess.
Speaker Leonardsays : Um, eight.  Point four.
Speaker Gablehousersays : I’m sorry, that’s incorrect. AA, if you can answer correctly, the match is yours.
Speaker Howardsays : He doesn’t have it. He’s got squat.
Speaker Gablehousersays : AA, I need your answer.
Speaker Janitorsays : The answer is minus eight by alpha.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Hang on, hang on a second, that’s not our answer. What are you doing?
Speaker Janitorsays : Answering question. Winning physics bowl.
Speaker Sheldonsays : How do you know anything about physics?
Speaker Janitorsays : Here I am janitor, in former Soviet Union I am physicist. Leningrad Politechnica. Go Polar Bears.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Well that’s a delightful little story, but our arrangement was that you sit here and not say anything, I answer the questions.
Speaker Janitorsays : You didn’t answer question.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Hey look, now maybe you have democracy now in your beloved Russia, but on this physics bowl team I rule with an iron fist. . Ow!
Speaker Gablehousersays : AA, I need your official answer.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Well it’s not what he said.
Speaker Gablehousersays : Then what is it?
Speaker Sheldonsays : I want a different question.
Speaker Gablehousersays : You can’t have a different question.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Formal protest.
Speaker Gablehousersays : Denied.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Informal protest.
Speaker Gablehousersays : Denied. I need your official answer.
Speaker Sheldonsays : No. I decline to provide one.
Speaker Gablehousersays : Well, that’s too bad, because the answer your teammate gave was correct.
Speaker Sheldonsays : That’s your opinion.
Speaker Gablehousersays : Alright, the winner of the match is…
Speaker Leonardsays : Hang on. Sheldon, is proving that you are single-handedly smarter than everyone else so important that you would rather lose by yourself than win as part of a team.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I don’t understand the question.
Speaker Leonardsays : Go ahead.
Speaker Gablehousersays : The winner is PMS!

[{"role": "user", "content": "Context:
November 16, 2009, Session #357 Turn 22
Speaker Pennysays : Hey, Sheldon, Sheldon, look, I am scared and in a lot of pain. Could you please just take a break from being you for just a minute and try being, I don’t know, comforting?
November 27, 2009, Session #363 Turn 8
Speaker Rajsays : Oh, Sheldon, you remind me of a young Lex Luthor.
August 28, 2008, Session #160 Turn 65
Speaker Sheldonsays : While others mourned Lucky, I realised his untimely demise provided me with the opportunity to replace him with something more suited to my pet needs. A faithful companion that I could snuggle with at night, and yet would be capable of killing upon telepathic command.
December 15, 2008, Session #214 Turn 32
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh, Penny. I know you think you’re being generous, but the foundation of gift-giving is reciprocity. You haven’t given me a gift, you’ve given me an obligation.
January 23, 2009, Session #232 Turn 28
Speaker Sheldonsays : Penny, Penny, Penny. Everything you’re saying is true, but please allow me to continue. Raj, you’re out. The good question, while you do provide a certain cultural diversity to an otherwise homogenous group, your responses to the questionnaire were truly disturbing. How could you, for a moment, think that my favourite amino acid is Glutamine?

raw_source_record.dialog_embedder_dialog:
[{"role": "user", "content": "Context:
Coversation [November 5, 2007, Session #49]:
Speaker Pennysays : Ugh, I cannot believe Christie let Howard into my apartment.
Speaker Sheldonsays : And I cannot believe people pay for horoscopes, but on a more serious note it’s 8:13 and we’re still not playing Halo.
Speaker Leonardsays : Okay, fine, we’ll just play one on one until he gets back.
Speaker Sheldonsays : One on one? We don’t play one on one, we play teams, not one on one. One on one!
Speaker Leonardsays : Well the only way we can play teams at this point is if we cut Raj in half.
Speaker Rajsays : Oh, sure, cut the foreigner in half, there’s a billion more where he came from.
Speaker Pennysays : Hey, if you guys need a fourth, I’ll play.
Speaker Leonardsays : Great idea.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Uh, no. The wheel was a great idea. Relativity was a great idea. This is a notion, and a rather sucky one at that.
Speaker Pennysays : Why?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Why? Oh, Penny, Penny, Penny.
Speaker Pennysays : Oh, what, what, what?
Speaker Sheldonsays : This is a complex battle simulation with a steep learning curve, there are myriad weapons, vehicles and strategies to master, not to mention an extremely intricate back-story.
Speaker television)says : Oh cool, whose head did I just blow off?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Mine.
Speaker Pennysays : Okay, I got this, lock and load boys.
Speaker Leonardsays : It’s the only way we can play teams.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Yes, but whoever’s her partner will be hamstrung by her lack of experience and not to mention that fact that sh….
Speaker Pennysays : Ha-ha, there goes your head again.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Okay, this isn’t at all good sportsmanship to shoot somebody who’s just respawned, you need to give them a chance to  now come on!
Speaker Sheldonsays : Raj, Raj, she’s got me cornered, cover me.
Speaker Pennysays : Cover this, suckers. Ha-ha-ha!
Speaker Leonardsays : Penny, you are on fire.
Speaker Pennysays : Yeah, so is Sheldon.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Okay, that’s it, I don’t know how, but she is cheating. No-one can be that attractive and be this skilled at a video game.
Speaker Pennysays : Wait, wait, Sheldon, come back, you forgot something.
Speaker Sheldonsays : What?
Speaker Pennysays : This plasma grenade.  Ha! Look, it’s raining you!
Speaker Sheldonsays : You laugh now, you just wait until you need tech support.
Speaker Pennysays : Gosh, he’s kind of a sore loser, isn’t he?
Speaker Leonardsays : Well, to be fair, he is also a rather unpleasant winner.
Speaker Pennysays : Well, it’s been fun.
Speaker Leonardsays : You know, Penny, we make such a good team, maybe we could enter a couple of Halo tournaments sometime.
Speaker Pennysays : Or we could just have a life.
Speaker Leonardsays : I guess for you that’s an option.
Speaker Pennysays : Good night, Leonard.
Speaker Leonardsays : Good night.
Speaker Pennysays : As usual, nice talking to you Raj
Speaker Rajsays : What do you suppose she meant by that?
Speaker Leonardsays : She’s an enigma, Raj.
Speaker Sheldonsays : And another thing, there’s a certain ethic to the game, Penny, a well estab…
Speaker Leonardsays : She’s gone, Sheldon.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Well she could have said goodbye.
Speaker again)says : Okay, I have a problem.
Speaker Sheldonsays : It’s called carpal tunnel syndrome, and quite frankly you deserve it.
Speaker Leonardsays : What’s wrong?
Speaker Pennysays : Um, well, Howard and Christie are… kind of… hooking up in my bedroom.
Speaker Leonardsays : Are you sure?
Speaker Pennysays : Look, I grew up on a farm, okay, from what I heard they’re either having sex or Howard’s caught in a milking machine. Do you mind if I stay here tonight?
Speaker Leonardsays : No, take the couch, or my bed, I just got new pillows, hypo-allergenics.
Speaker Pennysays : Uh, the couch is good.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Hold that thought, Leonard, a moment.
Speaker Leonardsays : Let me guess, you have a problem with this.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Where do I begin?
Speaker Leonardsays : It’s up to you, crazy person’s choice.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Well first, we don’t have house guests, frankly if I could afford the rent I’d ask you to leave.
Speaker Leonardsays : Your friendship means a lot to me as well, what else?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Well, our earthquake supplies, we have a two day, two man kit.
Speaker Leonardsays : So?
Speaker Sheldonsays : So, if there is an earthquake and the three of us are trapped here, we could be out of food by tomorrow afternoon.
Speaker Leonardsays : I’m sorry, are you suggesting that if we let Penny stay we might succumb to cannibalism?
Speaker Sheldonsays : No-one ever thinks it will happen until it does.
Speaker Leonardsays : Penny, if you promise not to chew the flesh off our bones while we sleep, you can stay.
Speaker Pennysays : Hu.. what?
Speaker Sheldonsays : He’s engaging in reductio-ad-absurdum. It’s the logical fallacy of extending someone’s argument to ridiculous proportions and then criticising the result, and I do not appreciate it.
Speaker Leonardsays : I’ll get you a blanket and a pillow.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Okay, well since I’m obviously being ignored here, let’s go over the morning schedule, I use the bathroom from 7 to 7:20, plan your ablutions and bodily functions accordingly.
Speaker Pennysays : How am I supposed to plan my bodily functions?
Speaker Sheldonsays : I suggest no liquids after 11pm.
Speaker Leonardsays : Here you go.
Speaker Pennysays : Thanks Leonard.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Hmmph, wrong.
Speaker Pennysays : I’m listening.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Your head goes on the other end.
Speaker Pennysays : Why?
Speaker Sheldonsays : It’s culturally universal, a bed, even a temporary bed, is always oriented with the headboard away from the door. It serves the ancient imperative of protecting oneself against marauders.
Speaker Pennysays : I’ll risk it.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Hm!
Speaker Pennysays : Anything else I should know.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Yes. If you use my toothbrush I’ll jump out that window. Please don’t come to my funeral. Have a good night.
Speaker Leonardsays : Sorry about that.
Speaker Pennysays : That’s okay.
Speaker Leonardsays : FYI, his toothbrush is the red one in the plexiglass case under the UV light.
Speaker Pennysays : Got it.
Speaker Leonardsays : Well, sleep tight.
Speaker Pennysays : Thanks.
Speaker Leonardsays : Funny expression, sleep tight. It refers to the early construction of beds which featured a mattress suspended on interlocking ropes which would occasionally… sleep tight.

Coversation [October 19, 2009, Session #331]:
Speaker Howardsays : All right, Raj has played his phantom warlord card, and I am going to back him up with my strangling vines. Choke on that, sucka.
Speaker Leonardsays : Okay, well, then I’ll just cut your vines with my ruby sword. That’s right, I did it. I cut ’em.
Speaker Pennysays : Um, I have a question.
Speaker Leonardsays : Warlord beats troll, troll beats elf, elf beats water sprite, and basically everything beats enchanted bunny.
Speaker Howardsays : Unless you have the carrot power.
Speaker Pennysays : Okay, I’ve got another question. When does this get fun?
Speaker Howardsays : Are we going to talk or are we going to play Mystic Warlords of Ka-ah?
Speaker Leonardsays : Just play a potion card.
Speaker Pennysays : Which one?
Speaker Sheldonsays : It doesn’t matter. You can’t possibly win.
Speaker Leonardsays : Sheldon, don’t ruin the game.
Speaker Pennysays : How could he ruin the game?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Given the cards that have already been played, Penny can only be holding necromancer potions, which are only effective against wraiths and day-walkers, and there are no more of either left to be drawn. The cards remaining in the undrawn stack are four fire weapons, a troll, two ogres and the jewel of Osiris.
Speaker Leonardsays : See? Ruined.
Speaker Pennysays : Sheldon, that is incredible.
Speaker Sheldonsays : From your vantage point, it certainly must seem so.
Speaker Leonardsays : Sheldon has kind of a photographic memory.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Photographic is a misnomer. I have an eidetic memory, as I’ve told you many times. Most recently last year during lunch on the afternoon of may seventh. You had turkey and complained it was dry.
Speaker Howardsays : Well, I guess game’s over.
Speaker Pennysays : Really? Oh, great. I mean, aw. Okay, I gotta go.
Speaker Leonardsays : Why?
Speaker Pennysays : Because the last me I didn’t go, I ended up playing Mystic Warlords of Ka.
Speaker Howardsays : Not Ka. Ka-ah.
Speaker Pennysays : Ba-eye.
Speaker Leonardsays : See ya. Still can’t believe she’s going out with me.
Speaker Rajsays : Nobody can.
Speaker Howardsays : That reminds me, I have a bone to pick with you.
Speaker Leonardsays : What?
Speaker Howardsays : You and I made a pact that if either of us ever got a hot girlfriend, that person would have his girlfriend hook the other guy up with one of her girlfriends.
Speaker Leonardsays : Yeah, I don’t remember that.
Speaker Sheldonsays : June 30th, 2004. Opening day of Spider-man 2 at the AMC Pasadena. They only had red icees, no blue.
Speaker Leonardsays : Oh, yeah.
Speaker Howardsays : So you’ve been with Penny for like a month and a half now. Where’s my shorty, Morty?
Speaker Leonardsays : Howard, you can’t hold me to that.
Speaker Howardsays : Why not?
Speaker Leonardsays : Because when I made that agreement, I didn’t think I’d ever have a hot girlfriend. And I was positive you never would.
Speaker Rajsays : Hey, how come I wasn’t part of this deal?
Speaker Sheldonsays : You had left the refreshment stand in order to indulge in your customary preemptive pre-show urination.
Speaker Rajsays : Oh, so that’s how it works? I have a teeny bladder and now I don’t get a hot girlfriend?
Speaker Howardsays : Yeah, Raj, that’s how it works.
Speaker Rajsays : Damn.

Coversation [November 9, 2009, Session #343]:
Speaker Pennysays : Wow, Sheldon, I cannot believe you made up your own game.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh, Research Lab is more than a game. It’s like the slogan says, the physics is theoretical, but the fun is real.
Speaker Leonardsays : We must not be playing it right.
Speaker Pennysays : All right, five. One, two, three, four, five. Oh, wow, look at that, my Department of Defence research grant is renewed.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh! Great roll! Now you can demolish your Soviet-style cyclotron and build the large Hadron Collider.
Speaker Pennysays : Yay.
Speaker Sheldonsays : You’re a natural at this, Penny. And as the first beta testers, you two’ll have quite the edge when we play with Wolowitz and Koothrappali on Sunday.
Speaker Leonardsays : Oh, gee, Sheldon, I don’t think we can play on Sunday.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Why not?
Speaker Leonardsays : Because of the reasons that Penny will now tell you. Penny?
Speaker Pennysays : Actually, I have to pick up my friend Justin from the airport.
Speaker Leonardsays : There you go, she has to pick up her friend Justin at the airport, aAnd I can’t play ’cause I’m going with her, right?
Speaker Pennysays : Um, yeah, if you want. I mean, there may not be room. He’s got a lot of stuff, like guitars and amplifiers.
Speaker Leonardsays : Wait. What are you talking about?
Speaker Pennysays : My friend, Justin.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Pay attention, Leonard. That’s why she can’t play on Sunday.
Speaker Leonardsays : Who is this Justin?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Your turn, Leonard.
Speaker Pennysays : I told you about him.
Speaker Leonardsays : No, you didn’t.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Roll the dice, Leonard.
Speaker Pennysays : Yeah, my friend from Omaha, plays the guitar. Anyway, he’s coming to L.A. looking for some session work, so I told him he could just crash on my couch for a few weeks.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Come on, Leonard, if you roll a six, Penny dies horribly in a nuclear meltdown. See what I mean when I say the fun is real?
Speaker Leonardsays : Hang on. Some guy is going to be sleeping on your couch?
Speaker Pennysays : He’s not some guy. He’s my friend.
Speaker Leonardsays : So by friend, do you mean friend friend, gay friend, or ex boyfriend who you’re now platonic with but still might have a thing for you friend”?
Speaker Pennysays : Well, he’s definitely not gay.
Speaker Leonardsays : Oh, a definitely not gay musician sleeping on my girlfriend’s couch. Yippee.
Speaker Pennysays : Okay, we went out a little bit, a long time ago, but we were never like going out.
Speaker Leonardsays : Okay, not to be pedantic, but the last I checked went out was in fact the past tense of going out, which I think we all know is a popular euphemism for saw each other naked.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I’ll just roll for you.
Speaker Pennysays : Do you have a problem with Justin staying with me?
Speaker Leonardsays : What was your first clue?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Uh-oh! Industrial accident.
Speaker Pennysays : You know what? Don’t talk to me like I’m an idiot.
Speaker Leonardsays : I’m not talking to you like you’re an idiot! I’m saying the whole idea is idiotic!
Speaker Sheldonsays : You accidentally stare at a helium-argon laser. Lose one turn and a retina.
Speaker Pennysays : How is that not talking to me like I’m an idiot? It’s my friend, it’s my couch, and it’s my freakin’ life!
Speaker Sheldonsays : It’s also your roll.
Speaker Leonardsays : You know what? It is your life. If you want to have some stupid guitarist stay on your couch, then fine. Why don’t you just rent some bunk beds and invite The Black Eyed Peas?
Speaker Pennysays : Hey, if I want to invite the entire line up of Lollapalooza to sleep in my apartment, I will, and it’s none of your business!
Speaker Leonardsays : Are you listening to yourself? Do you know how childish you sound right now?
Speaker Pennysays : Oh, now I’m a child? Well, at least I’m not an idiot anymore!
Speaker Leonardsays : The two aren’t mutually exclusive!
Speaker Pennysays : Oh, you are such a…  What the hell is he doing?
Speaker Leonardsays : He’s drowning us out. He doesn’t like fighting.
Speaker Pennysays : Sheldon, just stop, look, the fight is over. Oh, and FYI, you never even heard of The Black Eyed Peas until you met me!
Speaker Leonardsays : I heard of ’em. Didn’t know they were a band. Sheldon, she’s gone. You can turn off the blender.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Has your relationship reached its inevitable ugly end?
Speaker Leonardsays : No, we just had a little spat.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Look on the bright side. As the result of Penny’s forfeit, you have become the world’s first winner of Research Lab. Would you like a commemorative snow cone?

Coversation [July 25, 2008, Session #150]:
Speaker (whispering)says : Sheldon. (Sing-song) Shel-don.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Danger, danger.
Speaker Pennysays : No danger, look, it’s just me, Penny, look, I got to level 25 and reached Purple Lotus Swamp, right?
Speaker Sheldonsays : You’re in my bedroom.
Speaker Pennysays : Yeah. Leonard gave me an emergency key.
Speaker Sheldonsays : People can’t be in my bedroom.
Speaker Pennysays : Okay, well can we go talk in the living room?
Speaker Sheldonsays : I’m not wearing pyjama bottoms.
Speaker Pennysays : Why not?
Speaker Sheldonsays : I spilled grape juice.
Speaker Pennysays : Well, wear different pyjamas.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I can’t wear different pyjamas, these are my Monday pyjamas. Penny, people cannot be in my bedroom.
Speaker Pennysays : Okay, just tell me, is it too soon to join a quest to the Black Castle?
Speaker Sheldonsays : You were invited on a quest to the Black Castle?
Speaker Pennysays : Yeah, yeah, by some guys in Budapest, I’m just not sure it’s the right move for my character.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Of course it’s not, you’re only a level 25, the Hungarians are just using you for dragon fodder.
Speaker Pennysays : Really? Boy, you’d think you could trust a horde of Hungarian barbarians.
Speaker (off)says : Please Penny, enough, I have to sleep.
Speaker (off)says : Okay, well you were great, thanks. (Comes out door) Oh, hey Leonard, listen, don’t got in Sheldon’s room, he’s not wearing bottoms.
Speaker door)says : Sheldon, you want to catch me up again?

Coversation [August 3, 2008, Session #154]:
Speaker Pennysays : Okay, I’m at the gate to the Treasury of the Ancients, I’m going in.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Stay close to the wall. Avoid the mummies.
Speaker Pennysays : Got it.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I must say, you’re playing very well for a woman of 23?
Speaker Pennysays : 22.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Right. 22.
Speaker Pennysays : Oh, here come the mummies, which spell do I use? The hateful strike, or the frenzy stance?
Speaker Sheldonsays : What happened to the rest of your group?
Speaker Pennysays : I dumped them, they’re a bunch of wussies.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Frenzy stance.
Speaker Pennysays : Frenzy, frenzy, frenzy, frenzy, frenzy!
Speaker Sheldonsays : So listen, would you describe your ideal vacation as a wild adventure to unknown lands, or staying at home curled up with a good book?
Speaker Pennysays : What?
Speaker Sheldonsays : These are market research questions. I’m filling out the online registration for your game.
Speaker Pennysays : Oh, okay, wild adventure. Oh, frenzy stance isn’t working, die you undead mummy, die!
Speaker Sheldonsays : Drink a healing potion.
Speaker Pennysays : Thank you.
Speaker Sheldonsays : You’re welcome. Anyhow, on a scale of 1 to 5, with 1 being always initiated by him, and 5 being always initiated by you, how do you prefer your sexual encounters to begin?
Speaker Pennysays : That’s on the registration?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh yes, it’s quite extensive. But if we complete it, we get a free expansion pack, 75 additional quests.
Speaker Pennysays : Ooh, awesome, okay, I totally like to initiate I’m a big old five.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Good to know. Big old five.

supporting_material.dialog_context:
[{"role": "user", "content": "Context:
Coversation [November 17, 2011, Session #709]:
Speaker Leonardsays : Hey, can I talk to you about something? It’s a little awkward.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I know what this is about. Given the professional standstill you’re at, you’re wondering if this is the appropriate time to abandon your research and focus on teaching. Yes. And if I may suggest, consider changing disciplines. Yeah, to the humanities, perhaps history. One of the advantages of teaching history is that you don’t have to create things, you know, you just have to remember stuff that happened and then parrot it back. You could have fun with that.
Speaker Leonardsays : Yeah, that’s not it. Stuart’s kind of interested in Amy.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh, of course he is. She’s very interesting. Did you know, when she was 14, she severed the webbing between her own toes?
Speaker Leonardsays : No. He wanted me to find out if you’d have a problem with him asking her out.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I’m not sure how to respond, Leonard. I don’t own Amy. Can’t own a person. At least not since? 1863. President Lincoln freed the? Slaves! Come on Leonard, if you’re going to teach history, these are the kind of facts you’ll have to know.
Speaker Leonardsays : You know what? Never mind. I’m going to tell him it’s okay to ask her out.
Speaker Sheldonsays : The question is moot. There is no way that Stuart, an impoverished peddler of picture books, would be at all appealing to Amy Farrah Fowler, a noted neurobiologist capable of performing surgery on her own feet with nothing but nitrous oxide from cans of whipped cream as anesthesia.
Speaker Leonardsays : All right, but just for the record, I checked in with you to see how you’d feel about it.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Fine. The record shall so reflect. Now, getting back to the problem at hand, what to do with a washed-up experimental physicist.
Speaker Leonardsays : I am not washed-up.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh, Leonard. Lots of people love you and want to help you, but they can’t until you admit the problem.

Coversation [November 24, 2008, Session #199]:
Speaker Sheldonsays : Penny, hello.
Speaker Pennysays : Hey, Sheldon.
Speaker Sheldonsays : What is shaking?
Speaker Pennysays : I’m sorry?
Speaker Sheldonsays : It’s colloquial, a conversation opener. So, do you find the weather satisfying? Are you currently sharing in the triumph of some local sports team?
Speaker Pennysays : What’s wrong with you? you’re freaking me out.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I’m striking up a casual conversation with you. S’u’up?
Speaker Pennysays : Please don’t do that.
Speaker Sheldonsays : All right, But I’m given to understand that when you have something awkward to discuss with someone, it’s more palatable to preface it with banal chit chat.
Speaker Pennysays : So, this wasn’t the awkward part?
Speaker Sheldonsays : No.
Speaker Pennysays : Oh, all right. S’u’up?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh, good, I used that right. Anyway, you’re aware that Leonard has entered into a new romantic relationship which includes a sexual component?
Speaker Pennysays : Okay, feeling the awkward now.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Her name is Dr. Stephanie Barnett and she is a highly distinguished surgical resident at Freemont Memorial.
Speaker Pennysays : Yeah, Leonard told me.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Good. What he may have left out is how important this relationship is to me.
Speaker Pennysays : To you?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Yes, see, of the handful of women Leonard’s been involved with, she’s the only one I have ever found tolerable.
Speaker Pennysays : Well, what about me?
Speaker Sheldonsays : The statement stands for itself.
Speaker Pennysays : Well, aren’t you sweet?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Anyway, should you have any interaction with her, it would be most helpful that she not see you as a sexual rival.
Speaker Pennysays : Yeah, I think she’s pretty safe.
